sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `matcher` currently provides a conservative direct-comparison summary between exactly one query record and one target record. Epithema v1 compares the shared positional overlap without introducing gaps and reports identities, mismatches, compared length, identity percentage, and any input-length difference in a stable table form.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts exactly one local query sequence input and one local target sequence input. Each input must resolve to exactly one sequence record through the shared sequence loader.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a deterministic single-row summary table rather than a rendered local alignment. The table reports the query and target identifiers, compared length, input lengths, identity count, mismatch count, and overlap identity percentage.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema matcher`. Current Rust service coverage exercises committed singleton FASTA fixtures and locks down the expected direct-comparison counts for the overlap between `ACGT` and `ACT`.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The historical EMBOSS name suggests richer local-alignment behavior, but the current Epithema v1 implementation is deliberately narrower. It does not perform Waterman-Eggert local alignment yet; it performs ungapped positional comparison only.

artifacts.label: Matcher query FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed singleton FASTA fixture used as the query input.

artifacts.label: Matcher target FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed singleton FASTA fixture used as the target input.

examples.title: Compare two singleton FASTA records over their shared overlap
examples.description: Compares the committed `ACGT` query fixture against the `ACT` target fixture and reports the deterministic ungapped overlap statistics.

expected_outputs.label: Direct-comparison summary table
expected_outputs.description: A one-row table reporting compared length, identities, mismatches, and overlap identity percentage.
